capture.buttonTitle: Read where your windows are right now and turn that arrangement into a Layout.
capture.title: Capture current layout
capture.subtitle: Found {{count}} windows. Pick which to include, then save as a Layout.
capture.unidentified: Couldn't identify this app — it won't be included.
capture.urlsHint: Browser windows can't reveal their pages — add the URLs you want this window to open.

settings.title: Settings
settings.shareUsageHint: Sends anonymous, aggregate usage and crash diagnostics to help improve InstaDesk. No personal data, file names, or screen contents are ever sent. Takes full effect on next launch.
settings.licExpired: Trial expired — a license is required to continue
settings.scHint: Works from any app, even when InstaDesk isn't focused.
settings.scRebind: Click and press a new key combination
settings.scNeedMod: Use Ctrl, Alt, or ⊞ Win + a key
settings.themeHint: Light, Dark, or follow the operating system (System).
settings.defaultGridSizeHint: The grid size used for monitors InstaDesk hasn't seen before. Currently-configured monitors are auto-pinned to their existing size when this changes, so your assignments are never disturbed.
settings.windowMarginHint: Pixels of padding around each monitor's work area. Pulls snapped windows back from monitor edges to leave room for physical bezels. Adjacent windows on the same monitor still touch each other.
settings.dragSnapHint: Hold Shift while dragging any window and release it — InstaDesk snaps it to the zone under the cursor: edges = halves (left/right/top/bottom), corners = quadrants, center = maximize. Works on every monitor.

bottomBar.pickRegion: Pick a region in the popup window…
bottomBar.rejected: ⚠ "{{title}}" rejected snap — app enforces own placement. Try closing and relaunching it first.
bottomBar.snapTitle: Divvy-style snap — opens a grid popup on M{{monitor}} and snaps the last-focused window into the selected region.
bottomBar.minimizeAllTitle: Minimize every open window across all monitors. Apps run as administrator can't be controlled and are skipped.
bottomBar.maximizeAllTitle: Maximize every open window on its own monitor. Apps run as administrator can't be controlled and are skipped.
bottomBar.clearCurrentTitle: Clear all {{count}} assigned cells on M{{monitor}} AND reset its grid size to the global default. Other monitors are not affected.
bottomBar.clearAllGridsTitle: Clear every monitor's grid — removes all {{count}} assigned cells across all monitors and resets each grid to the global default. Saved Layouts are not affected.
bottomBar.clearAllGridsConfirmBody:
This removes every assigned cell on every monitor and resets each grid to the default size. Saved Layouts are not affected.

This can't be undone.
bottomBar.gridSelectTitle: Grid size for M{{monitor}}. Changing this will clear that monitor's cells (saved Layouts are not affected).
bottomBar.confirmExitEdit: and exit Edit mode for the current Layout
bottomBar.confirmUnaffected: Saved Layouts and Quick Presets are not affected.

monitor.noSavedLayouts: No saved layouts. Save one in the Layouts tab.
monitor.identifyMonitorsTitle: Flash each monitor's number on its physical screen for a few seconds, so you can match these tiles to your real displays.

apps.selectCells: Select Cells and Pick an App to enable assign
apps.clearCustomConfirm: Clear all Custom history items? This cannot be undone.
apps.resetArgsTitle: Clear override → revert these cells to the app's default args
apps.argsPlaceholderMixed: Mixed — type to overwrite all selected cells
apps.argsHintReplacesPost: . Include it in your override if you still need it.
apps.argsHintEmpty: App default is empty. Whatever you type here is appended to the launch command.
apps.argsHintDefault: Differentiates two regions of the same app (e.g. two File Explorer windows on distinct folders).
apps.hideTitle: Hide "{{label}}" from this list. Use the Show-hidden row below to bring it back. The catalog entry is preserved.
apps.hideHiddenTitle: Hide the hidden-seed entries again — they remain hidden by default.
apps.showHiddenTitle: Reveal the hidden catalog seeds inline. Turn on Edit to Restore individual ones.
apps.restoreAllConfirm: Restore all {{count}} hidden apps to the list?

urls.addUrlFirst: Add at least one URL to a tab group before saving.
urls.saved_one: Saved {{count}} URL group: {{names}}. Pick from App History to assign.
urls.saved_other: Saved {{count}} URL groups: {{names}}. Pick from App History to assign.

favorites.title: Favorites
favorites.none: No favorites yet. Click “+ Add Favorite” to add an App or a URL.

help.title: Help
help.subtitle: How InstaDesk works — a quick reference.
help.manualNote: Prefer a full illustrated guide? Open the complete PDF manual, with step-by-step screenshots.
help.feedbackNote: Found a bug or have an idea? Send us anonymous feedback.
help.feedbackPlaceholder: Tell us what happened or what you'd like…

quickStart.title: Quick start
quickStart.body:
• Pick a monitor in the left panel, then drag across the centre grid to select cells.
• In the Apps tab, choose an app and click “Assign to Selection” to place it in those cells.
• Save the arrangement as a Layout (Layouts tab → + New Layout).
• Click Apply on a Layout to launch every app and tile it into place.

grid.title: The grid & assigning apps
grid.body:
• Drag across cells to select a rectangular region, then assign an app to it.
• Change a monitor's grid density with the Grid selector in the bottom bar (e.g. 6×6, 8×8, 10×10).
• Give a cell custom launch arguments in the Apps tab — for example a folder path for File Explorer — to open different windows of the same app.
• Clear Present Grid clears the current monitor; Clear All Grids clears every monitor (with a confirmation).

apps.title: Apps, URLs & Favorites
apps.body:
• Apps: pick from the built-in catalog, or click Browse… to add any program's .exe.
• URLs: build a group of browser tabs, choose a browser (+ Add Browser detects the browsers installed on your PC), and save it as a launchable item.
• Favorites: keep your most-used apps and URLs one click away.
• App History remembers programs you've added so you can reuse them later.

layouts.title: Layouts
layouts.body:
• A Layout stores which apps go where, across all your monitors.
• Apply launches and tiles everything; Edit reopens it on the grid; Export / Import share it as a .json file.
• If a saved app has no program or URL configured, that one app is skipped (with a warning) and the rest still save.

quickPresets.title: Quick Presets
quickPresets.body:
• A Quick Preset bundles several Layouts so you can apply them all together in one click.
• Create and manage them from “Manage QPs” in the left panel, then pick one and click Apply.

snap.title: Snap
snap.body:
• Snap → M1 grabs the window you last used and snaps it into a region you choose — no Layout needed.
• A grid popup appears on the chosen monitor; click a region to place the window there.
• Some apps enforce their own position and may refuse to be moved.

monitorsSettings.title: Monitors & Settings
monitorsSettings.body:
• Use the Monitor selector to switch which display you're arranging; the Display Array shows how they're laid out.
• Settings lets you switch theme (Light / Dark / System), language (English / Español), the default grid size, and a bezel margin so tiled windows don't sit under your monitor frames.

troubleshooting.title: Tips & troubleshooting
troubleshooting.body:
• Some apps (certain CCTV / video tools) place their own windows and won't tile — close and relaunch them, or leave them out of the layout.
• Single-instance apps (e.g. Outlook, Teams) open just one window; assigning them to several cells won't create copies.
• Windows shortcuts (.lnk) can't be launched directly — point to the real .exe instead.
• If an app won't launch, check its path in the Apps tab and use Browse… to fix it.

layouts.title: Layouts
layouts.editingHint: — modify cells in the Apps tab, then save below
layouts.saveEmptyTitle: Grid is empty — assign cells first (or click Delete on the card to remove the layout)
layouts.saveOverwriteTitle: Overwrite {{path}} with the current grid state across all monitors
layouts.cancelTitle: Stop editing this layout. Grid contents are kept; saving from here on will create a NEW layout via the slot prompt below.
layouts.newLayoutTitleDisabled: Assign apps to grid cells first (Apps tab); switch monitors to build a multi-monitor layout
layouts.newLayoutTitleEditing: Save the current grid as a NEW Layout in a different slot (use 'Save changes to {{name}}' above to overwrite this one instead)
layouts.newLayoutTitleReady_one: Save grids from {{count}} monitor ({{cells}} cells total) as one preset
layouts.newLayoutTitleReady_other: Save grids from {{count}} monitors ({{cells}} cells total) as one preset
layouts.importTitle: Import a Layout from a previously-exported .json file. You'll be asked which slot (A–Z) to save it to.
layouts.emptyPre: No saved layouts yet. Build a grid in the Apps tab and click
layouts.emptyPost: above to save your first one.
layouts.showContentTitle: Show the layout content as a large overlay over the central grid
layouts.hideContentTitle: Hide the layout content overlay over the central grid
layouts.editTitle: Loads this layout into the grid across all monitors. Modify cells in the Apps tab, then click 'Save changes to this Layout' in the amber banner at the top of this pane.
layouts.exportTitle: Download this Layout as a .json file (shareable / backupable).
layouts.editingFlash_one: Editing "{{name}}" → {{count}} cell on {{monitors}}. Make changes in the Apps tab, then click "Save changes to {{name}}" above.
layouts.editingFlash_other: Editing "{{name}}" → {{count}} cells on {{monitors}}. Make changes in the Apps tab, then click "Save changes to {{name}}" above.
layouts.saveEmptyFlash: Cannot save "{{name}}" with an empty grid. Add assignments first or use Delete to remove the layout.
layouts.nothingToSave: Nothing to save after target resolution.
layouts.editCleared: Edit mode cleared. Grid contents kept; saving now will create a new Layout.
layouts.assignFirst: Assign apps to cells first (Apps tab → pick app → Assign to Selection). Switch monitors to build a layout that spans more than one.
layouts.invalidSlot: Invalid slot "{{input}}". Use a single letter A–Z.
layouts.namePrompt: Give this Layout a name (optional). Leave blank to use the default.
layouts.maxLayouts: You've reached the maximum of 26 saved Layouts. Delete one to add another.
layouts.renamePrompt: Enter a name for this Layout. Leave blank to use the default.

browseApp.title: Add Application
browseApp.browseHint: Browse opens an in-app folder picker (server-routed). Tauri build: native OS picker is used automatically.
browseApp.pathInputPlaceholder: <drives> — or type a path and press Enter
browseApp.goTitle: Navigate to the path you typed
browseApp.notLaunchableTitle: Not directly launchable — Save will block this. To open this file in a tile, use the catalog app + per-cell Launch args.
browseApp.errLnk: Shortcut files (.lnk) aren't supported — pick the underlying .exe instead. For File Explorer, use C:\Windows\explorer.exe (or just delete this Custom entry to fall back to the built-in catalog).
browseApp.errFolder:
"{{name}}" looks like a folder, not an application. To open a folder in a tile:

1) Cancel this dialog.
2) Pick the catalog 'File Explorer' app (yellow chip, System category) from the App History list.
3) Select your grid cell(s) and click 'Assign to Selection'.
4) In the 'Launch args for selection' input, paste the folder path:
   {{path}}
5) Click Apply. Save your Layout.
browseApp.appHintOther: the catalog app that handles this file type (or a Browse-picked .exe)
browseApp.errNotExe:
"{{name}}" isn't an executable (.exe/.bat/.cmd). Launching a file directly is unreliable — the agent can't track which window opens, and many apps (Notepad, browsers) collapse multiple file launches into tabs of one window.

Correct way to open this file in a tile:
1) Cancel this dialog.
2) Pick the {{hint}} from App History.
3) Assign to your grid cell(s).
4) In 'Launch args for selection', paste:  "{{path}}"
   (Include the double-quotes — the path may contain spaces.)
5) Click Apply, then Save your Layout.

addFavorite.title: Add Favorite

addFavorite.errPath: Enter an application path (e.g., C:\Program Files\App\app.exe)
addFavorite.confirmExt: Path does not end with .exe/.lnk/.bat/.cmd. Save anyway?
